channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Technical issues prevent access to Moorabbin weather report
item.description: The article contains a technical error message indicating that the website has blocked the user as a crawler bot. News Corp Australia uses software to manage crawler traffic, and readers experiencing ...

item.title: Canadian Forces Snowbirds Stage Final Hometown Show Before Extended Pause
item.description: The Canadian Forces Snowbirds, Canada's renowned aerobatic display team, performed their final hometown show in Regina before an extended pause. The event, held on July 11, 2026, marked a significant ...

item.title: Tragedy at Toronto Street Festival as Mass Shooting Claims Two Lives
item.description: A mass shooting occurred at the Salsa on St Clair street festival in Toronto, resulting in two fatalities and four injuries. Police confirmed the incident took place near St Clair Avenue W and Arlingt...

item.title: iran acknowledges pakistan's abstention in unsc vote on nuclear programme
item.description: iran's ambassador and permanent representative amir saeid iravani acknowledged pakistan's decision to abstain from a un security council (unsc) vote that allowed proceedings on iran's nuclear programm...

item.title: Malawian Nationals Await Repatriation Amid Supportive Camp Efforts
item.description: Hundreds of Malawian nationals have established a temporary camp outside the Malawian consulate in Sandton, awaiting buses to return home. Volunteers manage the camp, providing essential services like...

item.title: Satellite imagery highlights Inday and Habagat flooding impact in BARMM
item.description: Satellite maps have provided critical insights into the widespread flooding caused by the recent typhoons Inday and Habagat in the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region of Muslim Mindanao (BARMM). These images...

item.title: Swiggy faces regulatory action over unsafe food deliveries
item.description: Swiggy Instamart has been issued nine notices by the Food Safety and Standards Authority of India (FSSAI) after consumer complaints about delivering expired, spoiled, and contaminated food items. The ...

item.title: Shopify CEO warns of rising antisemitism and Jewish community concerns in Canada
item.description: Harley Finkelstein, president of Shopify, has expressed deep concerns about the growing antisemitism in Canada, citing repeated public confrontations with anti-Israel protesters. During a speaking eng...

item.title: Defense Secretary Teodoro advocates for increased military spending and stronger deterrence
item.description: Defense Secretary Gilberto Teodoro Jr. emphasized the need for stronger deterrence and a larger defense budget to protect the Philippines' maritime rights in the South China Sea. Ten years after the 2...

item.title: ADC Commends Oyo Schoolchildren Rescue, Calls for Federal Action on Borno and Kwara Abductions
item.description: The African Democratic Congress (ADC) has praised the successful rescue of schoolchildren, teachers, and other victims abducted in Oyo State after 56 days in captivity. However, they have urged the fe...

item.title: Master Hanz Cua's Feng Shui Insights for July 12, 2026
item.description: The article discusses the Feng Shui predictions by Master Hanz Cua for July 12, 2026, as shared through ABS-CBN's Lifestyle section. It highlights how the master's insights align with traditional prac...

item.title: England advances to World Cup semi-finals after Bellingham's crucial goal against Norway
item.description: England secured a dramatic 2-1 victory over Norway in a tense World Cup quarter-final at Miami's Hard Rock Stadium, thanks to a last-minute goal from Jude Bellingham. The match, played under swelterin...

item.title: SBS News confirms fake story falsely claims Labor minister insulted One Nation voters
item.description: A fabricated news article falsely alleging Labor Housing Minister Clare O'Neil insulted One Nation voters has been circulating on social media. The story, which includes a misused SBS News logo, claim...

item.title: Faith Kipyegon's Fourth-Place Finish at Monaco Diamond League: Injury Impact and Earnings
item.description: Faith Kipyegon, the triple Olympic champion, finished fourth in the 3,000m at the Monaco Diamond League on July 10, marking her second consecutive defeat due to a nagging injury. Despite the setback, ...

item.title: Protests Turn to Harassment and Intimidation in Johannesburg
item.description: The article details how peaceful demonstrations against undocumented migration in Johannesburg have escalated into acts of intimidation and confrontation. Groups gathered in areas like Alexandra, Katl...

item.title: IMF approves $443.8 million for Tanzania's economic reforms
item.description: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has approved $443.8 million for Tanzania following the completion of final reviews of its economic reform programs under the Extended Credit Facility (ECF) and th...

item.title: Omar Abdullah accuses BJP of offering MLA ₹20-30 crore and statehood for political switch; party demands evidence
item.description: Jammu and Kashmir Chief Minister Omar Abdullah has accused the BJP of approaching one of his National Conference MLAs with a lucrative offer, including ₹20-30 crore cash, a ministerial position, and p...

item.title: Nigerian Army rescues 46 hostages after 56 days in captivity
item.description: The Nigerian Army successfully rescued 39 pupils and 7 teachers after 56 days in captivity during a joint operation involving multiple security agencies. Led by Maj. Gen. Chinedu Nnebeife, the intelli...

item.title: Arrest of Man Suspected in Ann Widdecombe's Murder
item.description: A 28-year-old man has been arrested in South Yorkshire on suspicion of murdering Ann Widdecombe, a former Conservative minister and Reform UK spokesperson. The suspect, a white British national, is cu...

item.title: Kwara PDP Criticizes Abdulrazaq Over Woro Abduction Crisis
item.description: The Kwara State chapter of the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) is celebrating the successful rescue of 49 abducted school children and teachers in Oyo State, calling it a triumph of responsible leaders...
